facts_disclosed: Instagram collaboration with Grab Food — photo shoot March 8, 2025, 45,000 baht deal

turns.content: OKAY I HAVE BIG NEWS NA KA 🎉🎉🎉🎉 I am literally shaking right now I cannot believe what just happen!!
Whoa, that's a LOT of excitement! Tell me everything — what happened??

Grab Food want to collaborate with me!!! GRAB FOOD!! they send me an email today na ka. they want to feature me on their app — like a spotlight on my khao man gai. they say they been watching my Instagram and they love my content and my story!!

That's AMAZING! Grab Food is one of the biggest food delivery platforms in Southeast Asia — being featured on their app is massive exposure. This is the kind of brand deal that actually makes sense for you too, since it's directly connected to your food business.

What are the details? Did they tell you what the collaboration involves?

okay so they want to do a photo shoot at my cart na ka. professional photographer, video team, everything. they feature me as a 'local street food hero' or something like that on the Grab app and their social media. the photo shoot is March 8!! and the best part... 45,000 baht for the deal!! 🤑

45,000 baht for one collaboration — that's incredible! And it's a photo shoot AT your cart, which means they're showcasing you in your real environment, not some fake studio setup. That's so much more authentic.

March 8 gives you about 10 days to prepare. The "local street food hero" angle is perfect for your brand — it's exactly what you are. This could bring a huge wave of new customers through the Grab app. Are you planning to also offer delivery through Grab after the feature goes live?

I already on Grab delivery but not many order because my page is basic na ka. but if they FEATURE me it will be totally different!! think about all those people scrolling Grab at lunch time and they see my face and my story. arai wa I am going to need more chicken haha 😂

You might actually need to plan for a surge in orders! When Grab features a vendor, it usually drives a big spike in traffic for a few weeks at least. Have you thought about whether you can handle increased demand?

But first things first — March 8 prep. What's your cart looking like right now? They'll probably want it looking its best for the photos.

that's the thing na ka. my cart is a bit... well loved haha. it's clean but the paint is old and my sign is faded from the sun. I think I need to make it look nice for the photo shoot. maybe new sign?? maybe repaint?? but I don't want to spend too much because the 45,000 baht is supposed to be PROFIT not go back into making the cart pretty 😅

Haha the struggle between wanting to look good and wanting to keep the money! Here's the practical take: you don't need to do a full renovation. A few targeted upgrades go a long way on camera:

1. **New sign** — this is the one thing I'd spend on. A clean, bold sign with your name and maybe your Instagram handle. Probably 1,500-3,000 baht for a nice one. And you'll use it every day after the shoot anyway.
2. **Deep clean** — scrub everything, polish the metal parts. Free.
3. **Fresh tablecloth or covering** — if you use one. A few hundred baht.
4. **Skip the repaint** — "well loved" carts actually photograph well. They look authentic, which is the whole point.

Total investment: maybe 3,000-4,000 baht. That leaves you 41,000+ baht profit from the deal.

ooh good point about the 'well loved' look!! actually you are right, if my cart look too new and shiny it won't feel like real street food anymore. okay so new sign yes, deep clean yes, but keep the character of the cart. I like this plan. and adding my Instagram handle on the sign is SMART because everyone who see the Grab feature and then come to my cart will also follow me on Instagram!! my friend Pim do graphic design na ka, I ask her to help. she will probably do it cheap for me because I feed her free khao man gai every time she come haha 😂

The khao man gai-for-design-work trade is a pretty fair deal! Ask Pim to keep the sign clean and legible — your name, "Khao Man Gai" in Thai and English, and your Instagram handle.

So here's your full March 8 prep list:
- New sign with Instagram handle (ask Pim this week)
- Deep clean the cart — scrub and polish but keep the character
- Wear your best clean t-shirt and a fresh apron — look like YOU but polished
- Extra chicken and rice in case they want to film you cooking lots of plates
- Charge your phone for behind-the-scenes content of YOUR OWN from the shoot day

That last one is key — film the film crew filming you. That makes amazing Instagram content.

OMG film the film crew filming me!! that is so meta haha I love it!! 'behind the scenes of my Grab Food shoot' as a Reel. okay I have my plan na ka. I am so excited I don't think I can sleep tonight. 45,000 baht and Grab Food feature and it all started from my little cart and my Instagram. my mom will not believe this when I tell her 🥹🙏

Your mom is going to be so proud! From her teaching you the recipe at the cart to you getting featured on a major delivery platform — that's an amazing trajectory. And you earned every bit of it.

Now go try to sleep! I know you're buzzing but March 8 is going to be a big day and you've got 10 days of prep ahead. Tomorrow morning, text Pim about the sign, and start your deep clean this weekend. You've got this.

Congratulations, Pan — this is well deserved! 🙏🎉
